analysis_plan_ref.anchor: section 5.2 (endpoint, SESOI/TOST, power gate v3) + section 8 (gates I-1..I-6, promotion rule, verbatim kill criteria + envelope) + section 11 (outcome map)

dependent_vars.definition: Pooled-item accuracy over the T0 informative-task-filtered pinned pubeval set (acc_norm for MC, EM for gsm8k), per cell; retention = cell accuracy / A0 accuracy; all registered claims ride on raw paired accuracy deltas (§5.2).
dependent_vars.name: accuracy

dependent_vars.definition: Exact retained parameter count per cell incl. residual bridges (rotated arms) computed by the harness; matched compression = matched retained count (ASM-1657); never a width fraction.
dependent_vars.name: params

dependent_vars.definition: Measured packed artifact bytes per cell (Appendix B discipline: width != bytes).
dependent_vars.name: memory
dependent_vars.unit: bytes (packed safetensors, fp32; M2 also sparse-CSR)

dependent_vars.definition: Per-token forward FLOPs per cell with bridge FLOPs disclosed separately (§4) — the inference-cost note of the metric vector.
dependent_vars.name: inference_compute

dependent_vars.definition: Identically ZERO by construction — the campaign is training-free per the ASM-1667 definition (frozen-model forward passes + closed-form calibration algebra only); reported as 0 for every arm.
dependent_vars.name: training_compute

dependent_vars.definition: Delta* = min(paired pooled-item retention delta A1 vs M1, A1 vs mean-seed R1) per full-item cell; primary at rho=0.75/r135 (ASM-1703).
dependent_vars.name: delta_star

dependent_vars.definition: Out-of-kernel fluency guard per cell (ASM-1666), outside every accuracy aggregate; A1 inflation > 1.5x the better of M1/R1 breaches the guard (§8 promotion rule, §11 guard row).
dependent_vars.name: fluency_guard_gold_ppl

dependent_vars.definition: Measured per-contrast paired discordance q, recomputed in-flight; any claim with q > q_ref = 0.25 is flagged with its achieved power (ASM-1720).
dependent_vars.name: measured_discordance

n_planned.arm_grid_coverage: A1/A3/C1/C2/M1 full sweep; R1 3 seeds per r135 point (1 on r360); A2/C3/M2 at rho in {0.75,0.5}; r360 = {A1,A3,C1,C2,M1,R1} at rho in {0.9,0.75,0.5} (ASM-1654)

benchmark_items.note: registered pool = T0 informative-task filter output (donor >= chance+10pts, ASM-1655); subset tail cells (rho in {0.3,0.15}) use the pre-declared det_u 500-item per-task subset and are claim-ineligible (ASM-1703) T0 pin: folio = 204 (Yale-LILY/FOLIO GitHub v0.0 release; the gated HF mirror lists 203; see poc/pubeval/fetch_data.py).

endpoints.test: EXACTLY ONE primary (ASM-1703): Delta* = min(retention(A1)-retention(M1), retention(A1)-mean-seed retention(R1)) at rho=0.75 on r135, pooled items over the filtered task set, full-item cell only. Task-stratified paired bootstrap, B=10^4 resamples of pooled per-item records resampled within task, statistic = min of the two paired deltas recomputed on the SAME resampled multiset (joint/min statistic, never independent legs); PASS iff the one-sided 95% LB (/analysis/delta_star_lb95) > 0 (/analysis/primary_pass). SESOI = +/-3.0 pooled points; TOST = 90% percentile-bootstrap CI inside (-3.0, +3.0) (/analysis/tost_ci90); a wide-CI non-result is INCONCLUSIVE-underpowered, never equivalence. Point estimates at /analysis/delta_star_point, /analysis/delta_a1_m1_point, /analysis/delta_a1_r1_point; macro-average reported alongside at /analysis/macro_delta_star.

The ONE registered Holm step-down secondary family (§5.2, present members only, full-item cells only): (i) Delta* > 0 at rho in {0.9, 0.5} (/analysis/secondary_delta_star_by_rho); (ii) kernel-specificity A1-C1, A1-C2, A1-C3 at rho in {0.75, 0.5} (/analysis/kernel_specificity_ci); (iii) A2-A1 at rho in {0.75, 0.5} iff ddc0 passed (/analysis/a2_minus_a1_ci, /analysis/a2_ran); (iv) A3-A1 (/analysis/a3_minus_a1_ci); (v) r360 replication of the primary contrast iff S2 runs (/analysis/s2_replication_sign). Raw and Holm-adjusted p at /analysis/holm_family_p and /analysis/holm_adjusted_p.
TOST equivalence A1 vs C1 at rho=0.75/r135 (90% CI inside (-3.0,+3.0), ASM-1703): true reads out as the §11 corpus-steering-only row — the kernel added nothing over a generic corpus.
TOST equivalence A1 vs C2 (knull) at rho=0.75/r135: true reads out as the §11 definitional-corpus row — NSM grounding specifically added nothing over plain-dictionary content (MD-5 context).

Out-of-kernel fluency guard (ASM-1666): ARC-Easy gold_ppl ratio of A1 to the better of M1/R1 per full-item cell; breaches (> 1.5x) listed at /analysis/fluency_breached_cells — any A1 win in a breached cell gets the §11 guard-row wording, never a clean win. Curves at /analysis/retention_curves; chance-corrected reporting at /analysis/chance_corrected_retention (reported, never claim-carrying).

Measured paired discordance q per registered contrast, recomputed in-flight (ASM-1720); claims with q > q_ref = 0.25 flagged at /analysis/discordance_flags with achieved power from the pinned simulation grid.

Gate I-5 v3 (ASM-1720), booleans recomputed from the embedded poc/ddc/power_sim_ddc1.py raw powers (config.q_ref == 0.25, never quick_mode): superiority power >= 0.9 at true Delta = +3.0 on the joint min-statistic, MINIMUM over the dependence grid c in {0, 0.5, 1} (/gates/i5_superiority_power, attaining c* at /gates/i5_least_favourable_c, infeasible cells disclosed at /gates/i5_infeasible_cells) AND TOST equivalence power >= 0.9 at true Delta = 0 (/gates/i5_equivalence_power). Either leg failing at T0 blocks the run and surfaces MD-6.

S1->S2 promotion rule verbatim from §8: S2 runs iff the S1 primary claim holds OR any Holm-surviving secondary Delta* > 0 exists at rho >= 0.5 with A1 fluency-guard inflation <= 1.5x the better of M1/R1 at the same cell; r360 replication sign at /analysis/s2_replication_sign.

Opaque carry of the ddc0 max-stat outputs (winning method, admitted directions, t*) consumed by the A2 basis construction — recorded so the A2 cell provenance chains to the ddc0 readout (/analysis/a2_ran); kill/gate booleans at /analysis/kill_a_fired, /analysis/kill_b_fired, /analysis/kill_c_fired; I-6 energy-capture tripwire at /gates/i6_energy_capture_min and /gates/i6_flagged_cells (flags arm MD-4, never block); cost ledger at /analysis/cost_ledger.

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Results bind only to: SmolLM2-135M and -360M base checkpoints at the pinned revisions; training-free rotate-and-slice surgery under the §2.5 scheme; the pinned corpora and the filtered public-benchmark suite at the pinned poc/pubeval content hash (internal-relative numbers, never leaderboard-comparable); keep-fractions in {0.9, 0.75, 0.5, 0.3, 0.15}, registered claims from full-item cells only. Nothing here extends to recovery-trained compression, to other donors or scales, to other kernel states, or to deployment claims.
hypotheses: H1 (primary, task-mandated): at rho=0.75 on the 135M donor, Delta* = min(retention(A1)-retention(M1), retention(A1)-mean-seed retention(R1)) > 0 on pooled items over the filtered public-benchmark set — kernel-calibrated slicing retains more measured capability than BOTH the magnitude and random drops at matched compression (ASM-1703).
H2 (kernel-specificity, Holm family): retention(A1) > retention(C1), (C2), (C3) at rho in {0.75, 0.5} — only this elevates the result above published corpus-steering (§5.4).
H3 (geometry, only if ddc0 passes): retention(A2) > retention(A1) at rho in {0.75, 0.5} on 135M — kernel geometry, not merely kernel text, guides the drop.
H4 (hybrid content): retention(A3) != retention(A1) is attributable solely to calibration-corpus content (engine closures); never to symbolic machinery inside the weights (ASM-1659).
H0/NULL (TOST, SESOI +/-3.0 pooled points): A1, M1, R1 are pairwise equivalent across the viable full-item grid — no training-free kernel-guided advantage at this scale under this surgery.
kill_criterion_verbatim: ddc1 is killed if (a) gate I-1 fails for any rotated arm after one debugging iteration; or (b) at every full-item grid point ρ ≥ 0.5, retention(A1) < retention(M1) and retention(A1) < mean-seed retention(R1) (kernel guidance strictly worse than both controls where the surgery is literature-viable); or (c) the A0 filtered task set is empty at T0 (donor too weak for the instrument). A kill is reported at the same prominence as a pass.

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py

pins.harness_manifest: poc/ddc/power_sim_ddc1.py sha256=58dc71d6d1c3a7db9e514c66e3c17989cce5cfc0c5ef4e2699772d616826e1a6 (gate I-5 v3 power simulation, ASM-1720; pinned NOW alongside the analysis script; its embedded output enters the analysis sidecar and the I-5 booleans are recomputed from its raw powers, never trusted stored). poc/ddc surgery/eval harness (ddc_runner.py, ddc_surgery.py rotate-and-slice + LAW-1 provenance gate, ddc_selection.py, ddc_common.py, merge_shards.py, validate_mock.py, modal/modal_ddc.py) + poc/pubeval WITH the two ASM-1655 mechanical additions LANDED (per-item --log-items emission, aggregates byte-identical; BASE donor MODEL_REGISTRY entries, fail-closed ERR_UNPINNED_MODEL until the T0 revision pin): staged-bytes manifest sha256=c03ae3ea7dba042ecaeb03421fb737d6f41abd76ca88ae33c30b35e20b57d6a1 (canonical JSON; poc/ddc/modal/modal_ddc.py --print-manifest), green mock at $0 pinned as poc/ddc/results/mock-validation.json (monolithic == sharded+merged pinned-analysis parity over the 66-job canonical shard plan; ddc1 verdict mapping resolves on the planted mock gradient; LAW-1 static + runtime taint assertions green; per-tier dry-plans green under the $60 ceiling and the ddc0 $5 carve-out). T0 ops 2026-07-12: the five calibration corpora + probe fixture + det_u eval subset landed under data/ddc-* (builders poc/ddc/t0/, gate I-3 parity green) and the BASE donor revisions are pinned; staged sha re-pinned accordingly; pinned f2b Modal image lineage (requirements-image sha 0fac7243...).

anchors: section 5 (instrument, endpoint, SESOI/TOST, power gate v3)
section 8 (gates, kill criteria, envelope — verbatim here)

title: DDC arm campaign: training-free kernel-guided dimension collapse (PDC on the SliceGPT rotate-and-slice scaffold) on SmolLM2-135M/-360M BASE — does choosing WHICH residual directions to keep using the kernel (A1 static corpus; A2 geometry, G0-gated; A3 hybrid incl. engine closures) preserve more pinned public-benchmark capability than magnitude (M1/M2), random (R1), generic-text (C1), plain-dictionary knull (C2), and shuffled-kernel (C3) controls at matched retained parameter count? Design: docs/next/design/DDC.md (REWORK + re-review residual fixes applied; ASM-1650/1651/1653/1654/1655/1657/1658/1659/1660/1661/1664/1665/1666/1667, ASM-1700..1706, ASM-1720).
